landing.bio: I am an Applied Mathematics Ph.D. student, researching on human cooperation, particularly within hierarchical societies.

experience.aboutWork: CRM, sales, internal network maintenance, etc.

programming_skills.name: Programming
programming_skills.tools: Python, R, C/C++, MATLAB, SQL (mainly PostgreSQL

programming_skills.name: Mathematical Development
programming_skills.tools: Machine Learning, Neural Networks, Deep Learning

programming_skills.name: Data Analysis
programming_skills.tools: Numpy, Pandas, Plotly, Matplotlib and Seaborn, TensorFlow, Scikit, Keras, PyTorch,

writing_skills.name: Writing/Editing Courses
writing_skills.tools: Writing in the Sciences (Stanford), Academic Writing in English (UC3M)

writing_skills.name: Experience
writing_skills.tools: Reviewer for several scientific journals (PLOS One and Scientific Reports)

interests.paragraphOne: Apart from being a Ph.D. student, I enjoy most of my free time being outdoors. I enjoy biking, doing crossfit, and recently I started doing yoga.
interests.paragraphTwo: When indoors, I read A LOT and sometimes I write. I restore old, broken laptops to give them a new life! I also paint.

publications.title: Cooperation, social norms, and hierarchical societies
publications.authors: Pablo Lozano, Sergey Gavrilets & Angel Sánchez

publications.title: Shall we turn off the media?  Global information can destroy local cooperation in the one-dimensional ring
Cooperation on dynamic networks within an uncertain reputation environment
publications.authors: Pablo Lozano, Alberto Antonioni, Marco Tomassini & Angel Sánchez.
